description: This figure presents X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of Fe–10Mn–10Al–0.7C low-density steel comparing the hot-rolled (HR) condition with specimens annealed at temperatures ranging from 700°C to 1200°C for 1 hour. The stacked diffractograms display intensity versus 2θ (30–90°), with each curve offset vertically for clarity. Key phases identified include α-ferrite (BCC), γ-austenite (FCC), and κ-carbide (perovskite structure). At 800°C, distinct peaks for all three phases are visible, while at 700°C, κ-carbide peaks are present but γ-phase peaks are absent, indicating a nearly complete γ→α+κ eutectoid transformation. The HR specimen shows only α and γ peaks without detectable κ-carbide reflections, attributed to low volume fraction or coherency with the α-matrix. As annealing temperature increases to 900°C and above, κ-carbide peaks disappear, indicating dissolution into the matrix, leaving only α+γ dual-phase structures at higher temperatures. The caption and text report calculated lattice parameters: α-phase (2.89–2.91 Å), γ-phase (3.65–3.67 Å), and κ-carbide (3.76–3.79 Å), noting lattice expansion due to Mn, Al, and C solid solution. This figure provides critical evidence for the phase evolution sequence and κ-carbide dissolution temperature range (850–900°C), supporting the correlation between annealing temperature, microstructural constituents, and subsequent mechanical property variations.
